summary.intent: Implement AlertDialog as a dedicated agnostic overlay core in ars-components (issue #260), reusing Dialog's state machine, and then refactor overlay snapshot directories to match the per-component directory structure used by the input category.
summary.outcome: AlertDialog core was fully implemented with unit tests, snapshots (22 new), proptests, mutation testing (0 survivors after two passes), spec updates, and conformance test. Overlay modules were converted from flat `.rs` files to directory-backed modules (`overlay/<component>/mod.rs`) with per-component `snapshots/` dirs. PR #660 was opened, Codex review findings addressed, and a fix commit landed.

repo: The `input` category pattern is the canonical snapshot layout: each component is a directory-backed module at `<category>/<component>/mod.rs` with snapshots under `<category>/<component>/snapshots/`. Overlay previously used flat files and a shared `overlay/snapshots/` dir.
Snapshot-count lint has a 500-file workspace-wide warning threshold and a 40-file per-component hard cap. Total was 505 after this PR.
`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate; it runs fmt, check, clippy -D warnings, unit tests via nextest, integration tests, and adapter tests.
PRs with `.snap` file changes must have the `snapshot-reviewed` label applied; a CI check enforces this and will fail if the label is absent when the job runs.
`@codex review` triggers the automated review bot; approval is signaled by a 👍 reaction on the PR.
AGENTS.md enforces a hard stop before commit/push — explicit user approval is required.
`cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` fails on pre-existing unreferenced `utility/highlight` snapshots unrelated to this work; `--unreferenced=warn` must be used instead.

code.finding: `dialog::Context` carries `dialog::Messages`, so AlertDialog cannot use `pub type Context = dialog::Context` — it needs its own `Context` struct to hold localized `cancel_label`/`confirm_label`. State, Event, and Effect work fine as type aliases.

Snapshot path for a flat overlay module can be overridden per-test using `insta::with_settings!({ snapshot_path => "../snapshots/dialog" })` — this is how the flat modules redirected snapshots before the directory-backed conversion.
The snapshot-count lint needed to be updated to detect `pub type State = ...` machine modules, which AlertDialog uses instead of defining its own State enum. Codex flagged this as a gap in review.

Spec example for AlertDialog was using stale callback types and implied a plain Dialog context. The spec was updated to document the AlertDialog-specific Context divergence and the correct attribute/callback contract.

workflow: Write failing tests before implementation (TDD) to confirm the exact compilation error before filling in types/logic.

Run `cargo mutants -p <crate> -f <file>` twice: once after initial implementation to find coverage gaps, and again after adding coverage tests to confirm 0 survivors. First pass had 29 missed mutants concentrated in prop-sync and registration idempotence.
Accept new insta snapshots with `cargo insta accept` (not `--accept` flag) after the first green compile; snapshots are generated as `.snap.new` files that need explicit acceptance.

The CI `Snapshot Change Check` can fail due to a timing race where the workflow checks for the `snapshot-reviewed` label before it has been applied. Re-running the failed job is the fix, not a code change.

When converting flat module files to directory-backed modules, all hard-coded path references in AGENTS.md, spec markdown, xtask, and snapshot `.snap` files must be updated (snap files contain `source` metadata with file paths).

friction: `cargo xci-fast` took extremely long — multiple 60-second poll intervals across fmt, check, clippy, nextest unit, integration, and adapter compilation phases.
Mutation testing required two full runs (first: 29 missed, second: 0 missed), each taking many minutes with no intermediate progress output.
CI Snapshot Change Check failed on first push due to a label-timing race — the job checked for `snapshot-reviewed` before the label API call completed.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` is unusable in this workspace due to pre-existing unreferenced `utility/highlight` snapshots, forcing use of `--unreferenced=warn` throughout.

The plan specified `pub type Context = dialog::Context` but Dialog's Context embeds Dialog's Messages type, making that alias impossible without losing AlertDialog's i18n contract. Required a design deviation and spec update.
Codex review loop required a separate follow-up commit to address two findings (xtask lint gap and spec example fix), extending the session after the PR was already open.
open_items: Pre-existing unreferenced `utility/highlight` snapshots need cleanup to unblock `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` usage.
Total workspace snapshot count is at 505, above the 500-file warning threshold — approaching limits that may require snapshot pruning or the hard cap being hit.
No adapter components for AlertDialog were implemented; adapter work is deferred to later issue-backed tasks.
AlertDialog `initial_focus: None` default leaves adapters to interpret the CancelTrigger focus behavior — this adapter contract is documented in spec but not enforced at the agnostic core level.
